emails.subject: Re: December Gas
emails.body: Jane, I have a few issues with SWG per their email below. 1. SWG says the price on Sitara 518455 (Day 7) should be $9.50 instead of $9.55. If this is correct, could you please change the price in Sitara? 2. On Sitara 386140, SWG says that the trigger amount should be calculated on the physical volume, not the scheduled volume. Can you please let me know which is correct and adjust in Sitara if necessary? Also, SWG will be shorting ENA $198,026.60 for liquidated damages on contract 96003533. Would you like me to fax the backup to Patti Sullivan as I did last month? I have one last question for you on contract 96003533. For November and December gas, SWG is paying on the last day of the month versus the 25th. I checked the contract and it does confirm this information. However, it is my understanding that SWG is a netout customer (they have been receivable only for quite some time). I have checked with contracts and credit and cannot find a netting agreement. Do you know if there is a netting agreement which might override the payment terms on this contract? I have a $2MM dollar invoice with a 1/25 due date that they will not be paying until 1/31. Thanks for your help Laurie Forwarded by Laurie Ellis/NA/Enron on 01/24/2001 03:51 PM "Nancy Frazier" <Nancy.Frazier@groupwise.swgas.com> on 01/23/2001 02:47:46 PM

emails.subject: Re: December Gas
emails.body: Re: December Gas price on the 7th was 9.55 I don't understand the trigger question. We need to get the back-up on the penalty. Patti needs to give you info regarding a penalty we wanto tocharge them for February. Please get with her ondetails.

emails.subject: Re: December Gas
emails.body: On the trigger, our system calculates as follows: NX1 (Jan 2000) $6.016 Trigger price $5.180 Trigger credit $0.836 Trigger credit $0.836 Scheduled volume x465,000 Credit to invoice $388,740.00 Southwest Gas is saying that the trigger credit should be on the physical flow, not the scheduled volume. Can you please advise. Thanks Laurie Jane M Tholt@ECT 01/25/2001 09:51 AM

emails.subject: Re: December Gas
emails.body: Jane, I still have two discrepancies with Southwest Gas which I need your help in resolving. 1. SWG says price on Sitara 518455 Day 7 should be $9.50 instead of $9.55 You have already confirmed with me that the price should be $9.55. I forwarded your email to my contact at SWG and she responded with the email below. Could you please call SWG trader Mark Anderson so that this can be resolved? 2. On Sitara 386140 SWG says trigger is calculated on physical, not scheduled, volume You have already confirmed with me that the trigger is based on scheduled volume and that you feel SWG will not agree. According to my contact at SWG, their trader Bill Brown confirmed with her that the trigger is based on physical volume. Can you please call Bill so that this discrepancy can be resolved? I appreciate your help in resolving these issues. Although I have already forwarded your responses to Nancy at SWG, she is being very insistent that SWG is correct and is requesting that the traders resolve these issues. Thanks again Laurie Forwarded by Laurie Ellis/NA/Enron on 01/30/2001 09:02 AM Laurie Ellis 01/25/2001 11:07 AM

emails.subject: Re: December Gas
emails.body: Re: December Gas This is not going to get resolved. They disagree with our view and are not going to pay on scheduled volumes. However, we are not going back off our view and will continue to bill based on scheduled volumes.
